response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an experienced polyglot engineer with a strong software craftsmanship background, but his profile is fundamentally misaligned with the role's core requirements. His primary expertise for the past decade is TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang — not Java or Kotlin. While Java appears in his skills list and was used briefly at Altom for robotic arms and mobile tooling circa 2016–2018, it has not featured in any recent or sustained capacity. There is no mention of Spring, Quarkus, Hibernate, or any JVM-centric enterprise frameworks, and no evidence of deep JVM internals knowledge. His closest relevant experience is a six-month stint at National Instruments contributing to a C-based IDE (not JVM), plus open-source language tooling projects targeting the D programming language. These demonstrate genuine interest in developer tooling, but are insufficient substitutes for the required Java/Kotlin/Spring depth.
strengths: Demonstrated interest in IDE internals and developer tooling through work at National Instruments on NI LabWindows/CVI and open-source projects (Trial test runner, Fluent-Asserts library)
Strong software craftsmanship practices — TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, code katas — applied consistently across multiple senior roles
Experience with large-scale, high-throughput systems (millions of HTTP requests/second at Dunnhumby), showing ability to reason about performance and architecture
concerns: No meaningful Java or Kotlin experience in any role from the last five-plus years; Java appears only in a peripheral context around 2016–2018 and is buried in the skills list
Zero mention of Spring, Quarkus, Hibernate, or any JVM enterprise framework across the entire resume — the central technical requirement of the role is entirely absent
Current and recent primary stack (TypeScript, Node.js, MCP tooling) is fundamentally misaligned with a JVM-focused IDE engineering role, making this a near-complete skills mismatch

key_factors.factor: IDE and language tooling adjacent experience
